description: This is Figure 2, a scatter plot presenting the density of cementite (structures Pnma and Pbnm) as a function of Cr and Mn mass content. Two data series are shown: Cr-bearing cementite (filled diamonds) and Mn-bearing cementite (open triangles). The vertical axis records density in Kg/m³ from 7000 to 7800, while the horizontal axis gives mass content from 0 to 1. The plot demonstrates that increasing either Cr or Mn content reduces cementite density. The Mn series spans a broad composition range, falling from approximately 7700 Kg/m³ at low content to about 7080 Kg/m³ near 0.95 mass content. The Cr series is limited to lower contents (below ~0.25), decreasing from ~7750 Kg/m³ to ~7580 Kg/m³. These measurements provide the basis for the cementite density correlations—equation (6) for Cr and equation (7) for Mn—used in the regression Model II for ferritic steels, establishing that Cr and Mn substitution significantly lowers cementite density and must be incorporated into phase-mixture density predictions.
